text: A graded 13-week training program based on the 10% rule does not reduce running-related injury incidence compared to a standard 8-week training program in novice runners
The incidence of running-related injuries in novice runners preparing for a 4-mile event is approximately 20% regardless of training program progression rate
The 10% weekly mileage increase rule does not provide injury prevention benefits over more aggressive progression in novice runners
Novice runners can progress from zero to completing a 4-mile (6.7 km) event within 8 weeks without increased injury risk compared to a 13-week buildup
Running-related injuries causing at least 1 week of restricted running affect roughly 1 in 5 novice runners during initial training
